fundamentals.name: Netflix, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Netflix, Inc. provides entertainment services worldwide. The company offers television (TV) series, documentaries, feature films, games, and live programming across various genres and languages. It also provides members the ability to receive streaming content through a host of internet-connected devices, including TVs, digital video players, TV set-top boxes, and mobile devices. Netflix, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Los Gatos, California.

judgment.summary: Strongly growing, highly profitable business with robust cash flow, trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 1.4%; next earnings date is 2026-07-16.
positives: Revenue growth 16.2% YoY and analyst estimates for 42.2% FY growth indicate strong top-line expansion.
Profit margin 28.5% and operating margin 32.3% demonstrate exceptional profitability.
Return on equity at 48.5% signifies efficient use of shareholder capital.
Free cash flow of $25.99B TTM provides significant financial flexibility.
Forward P/E of 22.78x is reasonable given the high growth and profitability.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 28.24x and PEG ratio of 1.72x suggest a high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 53.79% indicates moderate leverage.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 1.4%, a minor but notable deviation.
Beta of 1.55 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Netflix stock experienced a notable slide despite a generally rising market, with recent coverage highlighting shifts in its investment narrative around advertising, content, and deal discipline. While Bank of America reiterated a buy rating, the stock's recent performance suggests investor attention is mixed. The upcoming earnings report will be key to understanding if the company can reignite growth and justify its premium valuation.
